source.definition: Having or showing concern only for oneself and one's own interests, often disregarding others.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "selfish" is derived from the word "self" combined with the suffix "-ish," which forms adjectives indicating a characteristic or tendency.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „selfish” provine de la cuvântul „self” combinat cu sufixul „-ish”, care formează adjective ce indică o caracteristică sau o tendință.

target.definition: Persoană, die nur an sich selbst denkt und handelt.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word „egoist” was borrowed from the French „égoïste”, which in turn comes from the Latin pronoun „ego” (I).
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „egoist” a fost împrumutat din franceza „égoïste”, care provine din pronumele latin „ego” (eu).

examples.sourceText: He is very selfish and only thinks about himself.
examples.targetText: El este foarte egoist și se gândește doar la el.

examples.sourceText: A selfish action can harm others.
examples.targetText: O acțiune egoistă poate răni pe alții.

examples.sourceText: Being selfish means only seeing your own needs.
examples.targetText: A fi egoist înseamnă să vezi doar nevoile proprii.

learningTips.text: Remember 'selfish' by thinking of someone who only cares about 'self'—it's all about me!
The word 'selfish' contains 'self', which is easy to connect to the idea of focusing on oneself.
Think of the suffix '-ish' as describing a characteristic, so 'selfish' means having the quality of focusing on self.
Imagine someone saying 'Me, me, me!'—that's a perfect example of being selfish.
